log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 2100-2400 geng road [24pln-00356]: recommendation on site and design to demolish four existing commercial b
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 2100-2400 Geng Road [24PLN-00356]: Recommendation on Site and Design to Demolish Four Existing Commercial Buildings and Construct 65 Three-Story Buildings Containing 145 For Sale Townhome Units. Thirteen Percent (19) of the Units Would Be Deed Restricted to Low Income Households. The Project Also Includes a Conditional Use Permit for the Residential Use and a Vesting Tentative Map to Merge Two Parcels and Re-Subdivide For Condominium Purposes Which Council will Consider In Conjunction with the Site and Design Application. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on July 8, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt from CEQA in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15183 (Comprehensive Plan Consistency). Zoning District: ROLM (E)(D)(AD) -Research, Office and Limited Manufacturing (Embarcadero) with Site and Design and Automobile Dealership Combining Districts.
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish commercial buildings and construct 145 townhomes on Geng Road.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 3781 el camino real [24pln-00161]: recommendation on a major architectural review application to demolish c
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3781 El Camino Real [24PLN-00161]: Recommendation on a Major Architectural Review Application to Demolish Commercial and Residential Buildings Located at 3727-3737 & 3773-3783 El Camino Real, 378-400 Madeline Court, and 388 Curtner Avenue, and to Construct a Seven-Story, Multi-Family Residential Housing Development Project with 183 Units, 13 Percent of Which Would be Provided at a Rate Affordable to Low Income. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on February 15, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt Under AB 130 (Public Resources Code section 21080.66). Zoning Districts: CN (Commercial Neighborhood) and RM-30 (Multi-Family Residential).
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish and replace buildings on El Camino Real with 183 housing units.

log.key: endorsement of the connect bay area transit initiative public comment
log.title: Endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative Public Comment
log.draft: The City Council considers endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'considers endorsement' which introduces interpretive language not grounded in the official item. The official item states 'Endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative Public Comment' — which describes an agenda item about public comment on an endorsement, not a consideration of endorsement. The headline's phrasing obscures whether the item is deciding on endorsement, receiving public comment about endorsement, or reporting on endorsement, creating ambiguity that goes beyond what the official text directly supports.

log.key: quasi-judicial. 4103 old trace road [25pln-00296]: request for a streamlined housing development review to construct 16 dwelling units on ei
log.title: QUASI-JUDICIAL. 4103 Old Trace Road [25PLN-00296]: Request for a Streamlined Housing Development Review to Construct 16 Dwelling Units on Eight Total Lots, Including Eight Single-Family Residences and Eight Junior Accessory Dwelling Units. One Single Family Home and JADU would be Provided at Below Market Rate. A Separate Vesting Tentative Map Application (25PLN-00298) Has Been Filed for the Proposed Subdivision. The Project Requests Concessions and Waivers Pursuant to State Density Bonus Law. A Compliant Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application Was Filed on October 1, 2025. CEQA Status: Exempt Pursuant to Assembly Bill 130 (Public Resources Code section 21080.66). Zone District: RE (Residential Estate).
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to build 16 homes and accessory units on Old Trace Road.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states the ARB 'reviews a proposal to build 16 homes and accessory units' but the official item describes this as a 'QUASI-JUDICIAL' hearing on a specific application (25PLN-00296), not a general review of a proposal. The headline omits material context that this is a formal quasi-judicial proceeding on a filed application with specific conditions (below-market-rate requirement, density bonus requests, SB 330 compliance, CEQA exemption). While technically factual, the simplified framing as merely a 'review' of 'a proposal' understates the formal nature and specificity of the proceeding in a way that could mislead residents about what is being decided.

log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline cannot be evaluated for grounding and neutrality because the official agenda item text was not provided. Only the item title ('Appointment of 2026 Emergency Standby Council') and body designation ('City Council') are present, with no substantive details about what the appointment entails, who is being appointed, or what the emergency standby council does. Without the full official item text, compliance with both grounding and neutrality criteria cannot be verified.

log.key: staff recommends the utilities advisory commission recommend that the city council adopt a resolution approving the fy 2027 electric financi
log.title: Staff Recommends the Utilities Advisory Commission Recommend that the City Council Adopt a Resolution Approving the FY 2027 Electric Financial Forecast, including Approving a Reserve Transfers, and Amending Electric Rate Schedules E-1 (Residential Electric Service), E-1 TOU (Residential Time of Use Electric Service), E-2 (Residential Master-Metered and Small Non-Residential Electric Service), E-2-G (Residential Master-Metered and Small Non-Residential Green Power Electric Service), E-4 (Medium Non-Residential Electric Service), E-4-G (Medium Non-Residential Green Power Electric Service), E-4 TOU (Medium Non-Residential Time of Use Electric Service), E-7 (Large Non-Residential Electric Service), E-7-G (Large Non-Residential Green Power Electric Service), E-7 TOU (Large Non-Residential Time of Use Electric Service), E-14 (Street Lights), E-16 (Unmetered Electric Service), E-EEC-1 (Export Electricity Compensation), and E-NSE-1 (Net Metering Net Surplus Electricity Compensation); CEQA Status: Not a project. under CEQA Guidelines Section 15378(b)(5) (ACTION: 7:35PM – 8:35PM) Late Packet Report added, Staff Presentation
log.draft: The Utilities Advisory Commission reviews the city's electric utility budget and proposed rate changes.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'reviews' which implies a general oversight function, but the official item text shows the Commission is specifically being asked to 'Recommend that the City Council Adopt' a resolution. The headline omits the substantive content (electric rate schedule amendments across 14+ rate categories and a financial forecast), which could mislead readers about the scope and significance of the action. A fully grounded headline must capture that this is about recommending rate changes, not merely reviewing a budget.

log.title: Stormwater Management Fee Rate Increase – No Action Needed (5 min)
log.draft: The Stormwater Management Oversight Committee reviews a stormwater management fee rate increase.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states the committee 'reviews a stormwater management fee rate increase,' but the official item explicitly states 'No Action Needed.' A neutral headline must reflect that no action is being taken, not just that a review occurs. The current headline omits material information about the outcome/status and could mislead readers about what is actually happening at this meeting.

log.key: downtown parking garage - approval of conceptual design of artwork by amy landesberg and approval of funding in the amount of up to $237,000
log.title: Downtown Parking Garage - Approval of conceptual design of artwork by Amy Landesberg and approval of funding in the amount of up to $237,000 for the fabrication and delivery of artwork for the new Downtown Parking Garage.
log.draft: The Public Art Commission reviews artwork design and $237,000 in funding for the Downtown Parking Garage.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'reviews artwork design and $237,000 in funding' but the official item specifies 'Approval of conceptual design' and 'approval of funding.' The headline's use of 'reviews' obscures the action being taken (approval), which misrepresents the nature of the agenda item. An approval is a definitive action, not merely a review.

log.title: Review and Discussion of Scope of Policy Research and Analysis Planned for 2026-2027 S/CAP Work Plan Items CA13, CA14, and CA16 focused on the Regulatory and Financial Strategies and Scenarios for Community-wide Electrification; CEQA Status – Not a Project Late Packet Report Added, Staff Presentation

log.key: approval of minutes from april 6, 2026 meeting
log.title: Approval of Minutes from April 6, 2026 Meeting

log.key: 4075 el camino way [23pln-00202]: recommendation to decline adoption on second reading of an ordinance adopting an amendment to a planned co
log.title: 4075 El Camino Way [23PLN-00202]: Recommendation to Decline Adoption on Second Reading of an Ordinance Adopting an Amendment to a Planned Community Ordinance (PC-5116) to Allow for Modifications to an Existing 121-Unit Assisted Living and Memory Care Facility. First Reading Adoption was on March 16, 2026. CEQA Status: Exempt from the Provisions of the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15301 (Modifications to Existing Facilities). Supplemental Report Added
log.draft: The City Council votes on whether to reject modifications to a 121-unit assisted living facility.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'votes on whether to reject' but the official item describes this as a 'Recommendation to Decline Adoption on Second Reading.' These are not equivalent. The item is about whether to decline adoption of an ordinance (negative action on the amendment), not about voting on whether to reject modifications. The headline conflates the council's action on the ordinance with an action on the modifications themselves, which is an interpretive addition not directly supported by the text.

log.key: 751 waverley st [26pln‐00034]: request for director's review of an approved protected tree removal permit via over-the-counter planning enti
log.title: 751 Waverley St [26PLN‐00034]: Request for Director's Review of an approved Protected Tree Removal Permit via Over-the-Counter Planning Entitlement.
log.draft: The Public Works Director reviews a request to reconsider a tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'reconsider' which interprets the nature of the Director's Review as a reconsideration or reversal opportunity. The official item describes it as a 'Request for Director's Review' of an 'approved' permit—a formal review process with neutral procedural language. 'Reconsider' adds interpretive framing about the likely outcome or purpose that is not directly supported by the official text.

log.key: approval of second amendment to the lease between the city of palo alto and community skating, inc. for the tennis facility at 3005 middlefi
log.title: Approval of Second Amendment to the Lease Between the City of Palo Alto and Community Skating, Inc. for the Tennis Facility at 3005 Middlefield Road, Extending the Term to January 1, 2040; CEQA Status – Categorically Exempt
log.draft: The City Council reviews a lease extension for the tennis facility at 3005 Middlefield Road.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits a material fact from the official item: the leaseholder is 'Community Skating, Inc.,' not a generic tennis facility operator. The headline says 'reviews' (suggesting deliberation is ongoing) when the agenda item explicitly requests 'Approval,' indicating a specific action is proposed. These omissions create an incomplete picture of what the Council will actually vote on.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto amending chapter 9.73 (city policy against arbitrary discrimination) of ti
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Amending Chapter 9.73 (City Policy Against Arbitrary Discrimination) of Title 9 (Public Peace, Morals and Safety) of the Palo Alto Municipal Code to Add Sections 9.73.050 (Local CEDAW Principles) and 9.73.060 (Private Right of Action) and Amend Associated Sections (FIRST READING: March 23, 2026, PASSED: 7-0)
log.draft: The City Council approves changes to the anti-discrimination policy and adds a private right of action.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'approves changes' which is interpretive framing. The official item states this is 'SECOND READING' with a prior 'FIRST READING: March 23, 2026, PASSED: 7-0'—meaning the council already approved it at first reading. Second reading typically indicates a subsequent procedural step, not a new approval. The headline misrepresents the procedural stage and could confuse readers about what action occurred at this meeting versus prior action. Additionally, 'adds a private right of action' requires readers to understand technical legal terminology and implications without neutral context about what this means or its effects.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto creating new pamc chapter 9.05 (entertainment zones) and establishing an e
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Creating New PAMC Chapter 9.05 (Entertainment Zones) and Establishing an Entertainment Zone on a Portion of California Avenue (FIRST READING: April 20, 2026; PASSED: 7-0)
log.draft: The City Council approves an ordinance creating entertainment zones and designating part of California Avenue as one.

log.title: Update on Flock Automated License Plate Recognition Technology Public Comments

log.key: citywide internal support and administration (11:15 am – 12:00 pm) staff presentation a) city attorney’s office (o:147-156) b) city auditor’
log.title: Citywide Internal Support and Administration (11:15 AM – 12:00 PM) Staff Presentation a) City Attorney’s Office (O:147-156) b) City Auditor’s Office (O:157-162) c) City Clerk’s Office (O:163-173) d) City Manager’s Office (Including Non-Profit Workplan Phase 1 Grants) (O:179-193) e) City Council (O:175-178) f) Non-Departmental (O:517-523)
log.draft: The Finance Committee reviews budgets for the City Attorney, Auditor, Clerk, Manager, and Council offices.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits significant agenda items. The official agenda explicitly includes: (1) City Council, (2) Non-Departmental items, and (3) Non-Profit Workplan Phase 1 Grants. The headline states the committee 'reviews budgets for' only five offices/bodies but excludes these other listed items. This is incomplete and therefore not fully grounded in the official text.

The item states the proclamation was 'added' to the late packet, not that it is merely 'under consideration.' Additionally, 'honoring' in the headline mirrors language from the official item but the headline's framing of what the council is doing requires inference about procedural stage that goes beyond what the item explicitly states.

log.key: feedback on strategies for community-wide electrification to model for future city council consideration; ceqa status - not a project late p
log.title: Feedback on Strategies for Community-Wide Electrification to Model for Future City Council Consideration; CEQA Status - Not a Project Late Packet Report Added, Staff Presentation
log.draft: Committees review strategies for converting Palo Alto to electric power citywide.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states committees 'review strategies for converting Palo Alto to electric power citywide.' The official item describes 'Feedback on Strategies for Community-Wide Electrification to Model for Future City Council Consideration' — meaning these are strategies being modeled FOR FUTURE consideration, not strategies the committees are reviewing for implementation. The headline omits the conditional/modeling nature and could reasonably be read as implying a more definitive or advanced stage of decision-making than what the item actually describes. This is interpretive inflation of the scope.

log.title: Approval of Professional Services Contract Number C26196545 with Michael Baker International (MBI) for $116,000 in FY 2027 and a Not-to Exceed Amount of $464,000 to Provide Administration and Consulting Services for the City’s Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Program for a Term of Four-Years Through June 2030. CEQA Status: Exempt in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15061(b)(3).
log.draft: The City Council considers a four-year contract with Michael Baker International for community development grant administration services.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 929 laurel glenn dr [25pln-00005]: adoption of a record of land use action approving a major site and desig
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 929 Laurel Glenn Dr [25PLN-00005]: Adoption of a Record of Land Use Action Approving a Major Site and Design Application for the Construction of a New 6,581-Square-Foot Single-Family Residence and Associated Site Improvements on a Vacant 4.23-Acre Site. CEQA Status: Exempt Per CEQA Guidelines Section 15303. Zoning District: Open Space (OS).
log.draft: The City Council reviews a proposal to build a single-family home on Laurel Glenn Drive.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits material facts that contextualize the proposal: (1) the zoning district is 'Open Space,' which is a significant constraint that residents would need to understand the controversy; (2) the site is 4.23 acres, a substantial size; (3) this is a quasi-judicial hearing with a formal Record of Land Use Action and CEQA exemption determination, not a casual 'review.' A neutral headline must convey that this involves a variance or exception in an OS zone, as that is the core civic fact. The current headline could mislead readers about the routine nature of the proposal.

log.key: quasi-judicial. 3200 park/340 portage: final map and street naming [26pln-00009]: request for a final map to subdivide one existing 3.92-acr
log.title: QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3200 Park/340 Portage: Final Map and Street Naming [26PLN-00009]: Request for a Final Map to Subdivide One Existing 3.92-Acre Parcel for Condominium Purposes, Consistent with the Approved Development Agreement, and to Name the New Private Streets. Zone District: PC 5597. CEQA Status: Council Adopted Resolution 10123 Certifying the EIR for the Project on September 12, 2023 (SCH # 2021120444). Item Removed Off Agenda
log.draft: The City Council reviews a final map to subdivide a 3.92-acre parcel for condominiums.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits a critical fact: 'Item Removed Off Agenda.' A civic information headline must accurately convey that this item will NOT be reviewed/heard, as the official agenda explicitly states. The proposed headline misrepresents the status and could mislead residents about whether council action is occurring.

log.key: first reading: adoption of an ordinance to amend palo alto municipal code title 18 (zoning) section 18.18.120 (noncomplying uses and facilit
log.title: FIRST READING: Adoption of an Ordinance to Amend Palo Alto Municipal Code Title 18 (Zoning) Section 18.18.120 (Noncomplying Uses and Facilities) to Allow Existing Floor Area to be Replaced in New Buildings Without Increasing the Degree of Existing Non-Compliance as Recommended by the Economic Development Committee. CEQA Status: Exempt from CEQA per Section 15061(b)(3) (Common Sense Exemption).
log.draft: The City Council reviews changes to zoning rules for replacing floor area in existing nonconforming buildings.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto amending the sign code (pamc section 16.20.160) to allow certain signs in
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Amending the Sign Code (PAMC section 16.20.160) to Allow Certain Signs in Conjunction with Parklets (FIRST READING: June 1, 2025; PASSED: 6-1, Lu no)
log.draft: The City Council votes on allowing certain signs for parklets under city sign rules.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses future tense 'votes on' when the official item shows this is a SECOND READING that has already PASSED (6-1). The headline should use past tense or present tense reflecting the actual status, not future tense suggesting a vote is pending. This creates factual misalignment with the official record.

log.key: second reading and adoption of temporary ordinances implementing senate bill (sb) 79, and introduction and adoption of interim urgency ordin
log.title: SECOND READING and Adoption of Temporary Ordinances Implementing Senate Bill (SB) 79, and Introduction and Adoption of Interim Urgency Ordinances Excluding Certain Historic Resource Sites and Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) Eligible Sites from Government Code Section 65912.157 and Authorizing Corresponding Zoning Map Updates. CEQA Status: Exempt Under Government Code Section 65912.160(c)(2). Supplemental Report Added, Public Comment a. SECOND READING and Adoption of Temporary Ordinances Implementing SB 79 (Staff Recommendations 1 & 2) b. Introduction and Adoption of Interim Urgency Ordinances Excluding Certain Historic Resource Sites from Government Code Section 65912.157 (Staff Recommendation 3) c. Introduction and Adoption of Interim Urgency Ordinances Excluding Certain Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) Eligible Sites from Government Code Section 65912.157 (Staff Recommendation 4)
log.draft: The City Council adopts ordinances implementing state housing law with exceptions for historic and transit-oriented sites.

log.key: public hearing / quasi‐judicial. 2468 south court [25pln‐00224]: request for director’s review of a tentative approval of an individual revi
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI‐JUDICIAL. 2468 South Court [25PLN‐00224]: Request for Director’s Review of a Tentative Approval of an Individual Review Application to Allow Deconstruction of an Existing Single-Story Residence and a Detached Garage and to Allow the Construction of a New 2,481-Square-Foot Two-Story Single-Family Residence with a 1,512-Square-Foot Basement. An Attached JADU is also Shown for Reference but is Only Subject to Ministerial Building Permit Review. CEQA Status: Exempt from the Provisions of CEQA in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15303. Zoning District: R-1 (Single-Family Residential). Staff Presentation
log.draft: The Planning and Development Services Director reviews a request to demolish a house and garage and build a new two-story home at 2468 South Court.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 470 olive avenue [25pln-00228]: recommend city council adopt an ordinance amending the zoning map to change
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 470 Olive Avenue [25PLN-00228]: Recommend City Council Adopt an Ordinance Amending the Zoning Map to Change the Zoning District for 470 Olive Avenue from NV-R1 (Single-Family Residential) to NV-MXM (Medium Density Mixed Use) and Adopt a Resolution Changing the North Ventura Coordinated Area Plan (NVCAP) Land Use Designation from Low Density Residential to Medium Density Mixed Use to Align it with the NV-MXM zoning of the Adjacent and Connected Parcel 2951 El Camino Real. This Action Supports a Staff Determination to Add 470 Olive Avenue as a Housing Inventory Site to Address No Net Loss Requirements. CEQA Status: Statutorily Exempt per CEQA Guideline Section 21080.085. Supplemental Report Added, Staff Presentation 6:15 PM – 6:45 PM
log.draft: The Planning & Transportation Commission reviews a zoning change for 470 Olive Avenue.
